vulnerability.title: Enable Randomized Layout of Virtual Address Space
vulnerability.description: To set the runtime status of the kernel.randomize_va_space kernel parameter, run the following command: $ sudo sysctl -w kernel.randomize_va_space=2 To make sure that the setting is persistent, add the following line to a file in the directory /etc/sysctl.d: kernel.randomize_va_space = 2
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then # Comment out any occurrences of kernel.randomize_va_space from /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf files for f in /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf /run/sysctl.d/*.conf /usr/local/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf; do # skip systemd-sysctl symlink (/etc/sysctl.d/99-sysctl.conf -> /etc/sysctl.conf) if [[ "$(readlink -f "$f")" == "/etc/sysctl.conf" ]]; then continue; fi matching_list=$(grep -P '^(?!#).*[\s]*kernel.randomize_va_space.*$' $f | uniq ) if ! test -z "$matching_list"; then while IFS= read -r entry; do escaped_entry=$(sed -e 's|/|\\/|g' <<< "$entry") # comment out "kernel.randomize_va_space" matches to preserve user data sed -i --follow-symlinks "s/^${escaped_entry}$/# &/g" $f done <<< "$matching_list" fi done # # Set sysctl config f

triage_decision.reason: This finding involves enabling kernel address space layout randomization (ASLR), which is a standard security hardening measure. The remediation involves setting a sysctl parameter and ensuring persistence through /etc/sysctl.d. This is a low-risk change that does not affect authentication, networking, or filesystems in a way that would cause lockout or system breakage.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan would resolve the vulnerability by setting the kernel.randomize_va_space parameter to 2 and ensuring persistence. However, the approach could be more streamlined and robust. The remediation script provided in the vulnerability details is more comprehensive than the proposed plan, handling existing configuration files more carefully.
concerns: The plan doesn't specify which file in /etc/sysctl.d/ to create/modify, which could lead to inconsistency
No error handling or verification steps are included
The plan is less comprehensive than the provided remediation script which handles existing config files more carefully
suggested_improvements: Specify creating/modifying a specific file like /etc/sysctl.d/99-aslr.conf for consistency
Include verification steps to confirm the setting was applied
Consider using the more comprehensive remediation script provided in the vulnerability details
Add error handling for file operations
